data.title: ServiceNow Stock Faces Q1 with 'Death of SaaS' Wolves at the Door — Are Analysts Running for the Exits?
data.description:
Enterprise workflow platform provider ServiceNow's (NOW) shares have declined about 35% year-to-date, swept by the AI-driven "death of software-as-a-service (SaaS)" tide that wiped out almost $2 trillion from the sector as of early February. However, as the California-based company prepares to release its first-quarter 2026 earnings after market close today, analysts remain bullish on its AI growth story despite trimming their NOW price targets.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

AI Fears Grip ServiceNow Stock

The "death of SaaS" refers to fears that AI-native systems will render traditional software obsolete by eating into their revenue and forcing a switch from a per-user licensing model to an outcome-based one.

As a platform provider, ServiceNow offers capabilities that enable businesses to execute tasks such as automating their workflows, running performance analytics, and deploying artificial intelligence and machine learning. Its flagship AI product is Now Assist, which is built on its ServiceNow AI Platform and combines generative AI with workflow automation to help organizations improve their productivity.

TD Cowen Sees 'Solid Beat and Raise'

During the first three months of this year, analysts expect ServiceNow to have posted $0.97 in adjusted earnings per share. This marks a 20% year-over-year increase from an adjusted $0.81 after December's 5-for-1 stock split. They also see revenue climbing roughly 21% to $3.75 billion over the same period.

Last week, TD Cowen analyst Derrick Wood reaffirmed his Buy rating on ServiceNow shares, noting that his background checks with industry contacts suggest that the company's overall growth trends and adoption of its AI-related products are healthy.

Wood believes the company will report "a solid beat and raise," with shares bouncing higher considering investors' current weak sentiment towards the software sector. However, the analyst slashed his price target by 24%, from $185 to $140, predicting roughly 40% upside in the months ahead.

ServiceNow Remains AI Partner to Enterprises

Similarly, Truist Financial's Miller Jump cut his NOW price target by 29%, from $175 to $125, suggesting about 25% upside. However, Jump also maintained his Buy rating on the company's shares, noting that his background research indicates that many enterprises see ServiceNow as a partner in the AI integration efforts.

The analyst also sees gains for ServiceNow from vendor consolidation, that is, companies reducing the number of software vendors they use to concentrate on a few key platforms.

Is ServiceNow Stock Good to Buy?

Across Wall Street, ServiceNow's shares remain a Strong Buy based on analysts' consensus rating. This breaks down to 30 Buys, four Holds, and one Sell issued by 35 analysts over the past three months.

Moreover, the average NOW price target of $165.69 implies more than 65% upside from current trading levels.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Vanguard Just Split 5 ETFs — Why It Matters for Investors
data.description:
Five major ETFs from Vanguard underwent forward splits, effective before trading began on April 21. Announced in March, the move marks an important change for investors in 2026. While the fundamentals remain the same, the lower share prices may make these ETFs more accessible for retail traders.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Trade WDC with leverage

The five ETFs are:

  • Vanguard Information Technology ETF (VGT) will be split 8:1
  • Vanguard Growth ETF (VUG) will be split 6:1
  • Vanguard Mega Cap Growth ETF (MGK) will be split 5:1
  • Vanguard S&P 500 Growth ETF (VOOG) will be split 6:1
  • Vanguard Mid-Cap ETF (VO) will be split 4:1

For context, a forward ETF split is when an ETF increases the number of its shares and lowers the price per share, while keeping the total value of your investment unchanged.

Why It Matters for Investors

The forward ETF splits from Vanguard matter for investors because they make some of its most popular index funds more affordable for everyday investors. While a split doesn't change the actual value or performance of the ETF, it lowers the price per share, which can make it easier for retail traders to buy in smaller amounts. It can also improve trading liquidity and attract more participation in these funds.

For long-term investors, however, the key point is that the fundamentals of the ETFs remain unchanged.

Which Vanguard ETF Is the Best Buy for 2026?

Using TipRanks' ETF Comparison Tool, we've compared VGT, VUG, MGK, VOOG, and VO to help investors find the best Vanguard ETF for 2026.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Last-Minute Analyst Warning: 'Sell' Tesla Stock; Predicts Big Earnings Miss and 95% Downside Risk in 2026
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) is set to report its first-quarter results for 2026 today, and the atmosphere on Wall Street is tense. While many investors are hoping for a rebound, analyst Gordon Johnson of GLJ Research is sounding a loud alarm. The analyst maintained a Sell rating on the stock and a Street-low price target of just $24.86, implying about 95% downside from current levels.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Trade TSLA with leverage

Analyst Sees Results Falling Below Street Views

Johnson expects Tesla to miss Wall Street estimates across key metrics. While consensus calls for adjusted earnings of $0.35 per share on $22.08 billion in revenue, his model is lower on both fronts.

The analyst forecasts revenue of $20.44 billion, about 7% below Street estimates. Gross margin is seen at 16.1%, also below expectations of 17.7%. Adjusted earnings are projected at $0.29 per share, compared with a consensus of $0.35.

The analyst also expects weaker cash flow, with free cash flow estimated at negative $2.5 billion, worse than Wall Street's forecast. On a GAAP basis, he sees an operating loss and near-flat earnings, while the Street expects solid profit.

Looking beyond the quarter, Johnson's view remains far below consensus. He estimates Tesla's full-year 2026 revenue at $83.4 billion, nearly 30% below Wall Street expectations of $119.5 billion. He also expects a full-year operating loss of $2.1 billion, compared with Street estimates of nearly $9 billion in profit.

Street-Low Price Target Reflects Cautious View

Johnson maintained his $24.86 price target by applying a 25x multiple to his 2027 earnings estimate. He noted that this multiple already reflects strong market optimism and does not include value from unproven areas like autonomy or robotics.

Overall, the analyst believes expectations for Tesla remain too high heading into earnings, with risks tilted to the downside if results or outlook disappoint.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Daniel Ives Is Betting on These 2 US Rare Earth Stocks Amid the Push to Break China's Grip
data.description:
Over the coming year, the US and China will come to cross-purposes over the global rare earths supply chain. For decades now, rare earths have been essential to the world's digital economy, and they will only become more important going forward.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Trade USAR with leverage

The importance of rare earths is running across the bent toward economic protectionism. China has long been the world's leading source of rare earth minerals, and the Chinese government last year cut its rare earth export permissions from a 5,500 to 6,500 ton-per-month range down to just 1,300 tons per month. And next January, the US military has announced that it will implement a ban on Chinese rare earths from every stage of the US defense supply chain.

In practical terms, this means that the US is headed toward a 'de-China' thesis on an economically essential supply chain. As this reshoring trend gains steam, the logical winners are going to be US-based rare earth suppliers.

Wedbush analyst Daniel Ives is placing that bet. He's looking at two US rare earth stocks, tagging them as potentially strong investments. Using the TipRanks platform, we've looked up the details on Ives's picks; here is a closer look.

MP Materials (MP)

We'll start by looking at the only US-based rare earths company that operates across the full supply chain for these vital elements – from the initial mining to the processing to the advanced metallization and magnet manufacturing that puts rare earths into the economy. MP Materials is based in Nevada, a state with a long history in the mining industry, and its chief asset is the Mountain Pass mine in California, one of the world's richest deposits of rare earths.

Mountain Pass, located in San Bernardino County at an elevation of more than 4,700 feet, operated for some 75 years, but was idled in 2015. MP Materials was founded in 2017 with the explicit goal of restarting production and reviving the mine as a chief source of rare earths in the global market. Today, MP Materials produces more than 10% of the world's supply of rare earth metals.

The mine itself is a major open-pit operation, more than 2,500 feet by 3,000 feet in dimensions, some 600 feet deep, and holding 1.9 million metric tons of rare earth oxides. This is enough to support several decades of American rare earth production, and, importantly, the mine's ore is known to be high-grade, at 6% total rare earth oxides over the life of the mine.

Along with its mining operations, MP Materials is also a major producer of neodymium permanent magnets and associated products. This magnet technology, developed in 1982, is essential to modern electric motors, and MP Materials has facilities to produce neodymium-iron-boron (NdFeB) permanent magnets, as magnetic blocks and as alloy flakes. Rare earth magnets are vital in the electric vehicle, robotics, electronics, and defense/aerospace industries.

For Daniel Ives, writing up the Wedbush view on this rare earth stock, the key points are its size and position as the leading US producer. He writes of the company, "MP Materials is the only fully integrated rare earth producer in the United States, owning and operating the Mountain Pass Mine in California… Mountain Pass is one of only four mines supplying 80% of the world's rare earths and the only one on US soil, making it nearly irreplaceable in our view. It is the lowest-cost producer of rare earth concentrate outside of China, underpinned by the highest-grade bastnaesite deposit in the Western world. As rare earth supply chain independence has moved to the top of the US national security agenda amid escalating tensions with China, which controls approximately 60% of global mining and 90% of downstream processing and magnet manufacturing, MP has emerged as America's designated rare earth national champion."

Following from that stance, Ives rates the stock as an Outperform (Buy), with a $90 price target to suggest a gain of 37% on the one-year horizon. (To watch Ives' track record, click here)

All ten of the recent analyst reviews here are positive, giving MP a unanimous Strong Buy consensus rating on the Street. The shares are priced at $65.76, and the $78 average target price implies a 19% one-year upside. (See MP stock forecast)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: Bitcoin's Coinbase Premium Just Flashed the Most Bullish Signal Since $126,000 Record!
data.description:
The biggest players in the crypto market are making a massive comeback as a key indicator flashes a signal not seen in months. On Wednesday, April 22, 2026, Bitcoin (BTC-USD) climbed past $78,000, marking a 14% gain for the month. This surge is backed by a specific buying trend on Coinbase (COIN) that has now lasted for 14 straight days, which is the longest winning streak since the coin hit its all-time high of $126,000 last October.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

U.S. Buyers Fuel the Current Rally

American buyers are showing an aggressive interest in digital assets as they outpace traders in other parts of the world.

The Coinbase premium is a tool that experts watch for cues on whether U.S.-based investors, especially institutions, are actually buying. For the last two weeks, the price on Coinbase has consistently stayed higher than elsewhere. This persistent gap shows that demand from U.S. hedge funds, corporate treasuries, and major firms like Strategy (MSTR) is very high. In the past, strong buying from U.S. investors has been a feature of bull runs.

Coinbase Signal Tracks Institutional Demand

The gap between exchange prices reveals exactly who is behind the latest market move.

Since Coinbase is the main hub for regulated U.S. funds and ETFs, its price movements tell the real story of market activity. When the premium is green, it means these big institutions are being aggressive in purchasing BTC. This trend is a massive shift from earlier this year. Between mid-December and February, the premium stayed mostly negative, and the price of Bitcoin fell from $100,000 down to nearly $60,000. The fact that buyers are staying this active through geopolitical noise and the DeFi crisis suggests they believe the current rally has a lot more room to grow.

Data Patterns Suggest a Record Run for Bitcoin

The last time we saw a 14-day streak like this was in October 2025, right as the market pushed toward its record of $126,000. Traders are watching this metric because it serves as an early warning for institutional sentiment. With the premium holding steady even as Bitcoin nears $80,000, the data suggests that the heavy lifting is being done by American investors who are not afraid of the current price levels.

At the time of writing, Bitcoin's price is sitting at $78,106.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Meta (META) Has Bounced Hard, but the AI Story Says It Is Not Done Yet
data.description:
Meta (META) has bounced sharply, but I remain bullish as its artificial intelligence (AI) flywheel suggests the rally is not done yet. Even after a roughly 30% rebound from its March lows, the company's transition from a digital advertising giant into a broader AI-driven monetization platform is still gaining traction across ads, engagement, messaging, and devices. That ongoing shift is what continues to support further upside from here.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Meta already owns one of the largest attention platforms in the world through Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and Threads. What is changing now is how effectively it monetizes that attention. AI is improving ad targeting, increasing time spent, boosting Reels engagement, and helping Meta open up new revenue streams beyond traditional feed ads. In my view, that combination keeps the bull case intact.
data.author: Davit Kirakosyan

data.title: Tesla Will Report Q1 Earnings Today. Does Wall Street Expect TSLA Stock to Recover?
data.description:
Electric vehicle (EV) giant Tesla (TSLA) is scheduled to announce its first-quarter results after the market closes on Wednesday, April 22. TSLA stock is down 14% year-to-date. Wall Street is divided on Tesla stock. Several analysts are concerned about elevated capital spending and persistent weakness in the company's EV business amid intense competition and macro uncertainties. However, Tesla bulls are optimistic about the company's robotaxi business, full self-driving (FSD) tech, Optimus humanoid robot, and AI ambitions. Ahead of Q1 earnings, Wall Street's average price target indicates a modest upside potential.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Trade TSLA with leverage

Recently, Tesla stock jumped after CEO Elon Musk highlighted the company's progress on its forthcoming AI5 chip.

Meanwhile, Wall Street expects Tesla to report earnings per share (EPS) of $0.35 for Q1 2026, reflecting about 30% year-over-year growth. Revenue is projected to rise more than 15% to $22.27 billion from the prior-year quarter. As seen in the chart below, Tesla's Q1 deliveries rose 6.3% year-over-year to 358,023 EVs, but missed expectations and declined 14% from the previous quarter.

Investors will look forward to management's commentary on the EV demand backdrop, robotaxi expansion, and updates on Optimus and other AI projects.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Stock Market News, 4/22/26 – Futures Rise as Middle East Truce Holds, All Eyes on Tesla Earnings Today
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were higher early Wednesday as market sentiment improved. The gains follow President Trump's decision to extend the ceasefire with Iran, easing fears of an immediate escalation in the Middle East. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 Index (SPX) were up 0.67%, 0.45%, and 0.53%, respectively, at 5:30 a.m. EDT on April 22.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for QQQ bulls

After the market closed today, Trump extended the U.S. ceasefire with Iran, saying it is intended to give the Iranian leadership time to submit a "unified proposal" to end the ongoing conflict. Oil prices also rose at the time of writing, with Brent crude (CM:BZ) up 1.06% at $99.52 per barrel, while WTI crude (CM:CL) gained 1.27% to $90.78.

Tesla (TSLA) is set to report its first-quarter results after the market close today, with investors focused on profit margins and Elon Musk's commentary regarding the "Terafab" project and robotaxi timelines.

Major indices closed lower on Tuesday as investors waited for clarity on Middle East peace talks. The S&P 500, the Dow, and the Nasdaq each fell 0.6%. The Nasdaq briefly hit a record high for the fourth time in five sessions before pulling back.

In addition to Tesla, earnings reports are due from AT&T (T), Boeing (BA), Boston Scientific (BSX), Moody's (MCO), ServiceNow (NOW), and QuantumScape (QS) on Wednesday.

Elsewhere, European stocks rose slightly on Tuesday but traded near flat on Wednesday as early gains faded amid ongoing Middle East uncertainty.

Asia-Pacific Markets Traded Mixed

Asia-Pacific markets were mixed on Wednesday as traders weighed the Middle East outlook after President Donald Trump extended the U.S. ceasefire with Iran.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 1.22%, while Japan's Topix slipped 0.67%. The Nikkei, however, gained 0.40%. In China, the Shanghai Composite rose 0.52%, and the Shenzhen Component added 1.30%.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
